description: HP 438A dual-channel (sensor A and B) HP-IB power meter. Measures single-sensor power (A or B), ratio (A/B, B/A) and difference (A-B, B-A) in dBm, Watts, dB relative or percent relative. Uses an alphameric two-letter HP-IB command set. Commands marked as requiring numeric entry must be followed by the value and the EN (ENTER) program code. Identification returns 'HP438A,VERX.XX'.

identity.description: Identification query. When addressed to talk after ?ID, the meter returns the string 'HP438A,VERX.XX' (CR/LF, EOI), where VERX.XX is the firmware version.

commands.name: Sensor A measurement
commands.description: Select channel A as the single-sensor active measurement (Sensor A power).

commands.name: Sensor B measurement
commands.description: Select channel B as the single-sensor active measurement (Sensor B power).

commands.name: A/B ratio measurement
commands.description: Display the ratio of Sensor A to Sensor B (A/B) in dB or percent.

commands.name: B/A ratio measurement
commands.description: Display the ratio of Sensor B to Sensor A (B/A) in dB or percent.

commands.name: Sensor A minus Sensor B
commands.description: Display the difference measurement A-B in dBm or Watts.

commands.name: Sensor B minus Sensor A
commands.description: Display the difference measurement B-A in dBm or Watts.

commands.name: Set A (active entry channel A)
commands.description: Select channel A as the active entry channel so that subsequent per-sensor parameters (cal factor, offset, range, filter, limits) apply to sensor A. Front panel SET A.

commands.name: Set B (active entry channel B)
commands.description: Select channel B as the active entry channel so that subsequent per-sensor parameters (cal factor, offset, range, filter, limits) apply to sensor B. Front panel SET B.

commands.name: Cal Factor
commands.description: Enter the calibration factor (in percent) for the sensor on the active entry channel; compensates for sensor mismatch and effective efficiency versus frequency. Followed by value and EN. Pressing CAL FACTOR then ENTER with no data sets 100%.

commands.name: Cal Adj (reference calibration factor)
commands.description: Calibrate the meter and sensor to a reference. Enter the reference calibration factor (sensor cal factor at 50 MHz) in percent, followed by EN, to start the calibration cycle. A negative value calibrates to an external reference; a positive value calibrates to the internal 1.00 mW reference oscillator.

commands.name: Zero
commands.description: Initiate the zeroing routine for the active entry channel (no RF power applied). Each sensor must be zeroed separately.

commands.name: Auto Range
commands.description: Set the active entry channel to automatic ranging. PRESET default for both sensors.

commands.name: Manual Range
commands.description: Set a manual range (1-5) for the active entry channel. Range 1 is most sensitive, range 5 least. Each range is 10 dB. Followed by range number and EN. RM then EN with no number selects range hold.

commands.name: Range Hold
commands.description: Hold the current range while switching from auto to manual range mode (retains the auto range setting).

commands.name: Auto Filter
commands.description: Set the active entry channel to automatic digital filter (averaging) length selection. PRESET default for both sensors.

commands.name: Manual Filter
commands.description: Manually set the digital filter (averaging) length for the active entry channel. Enter a filter number 0-9; the filter length is 2 raised to that number (1 to 512 readings). Followed by number and EN. FM then EN with no number selects hold filter.

commands.name: Filter Hold
commands.description: Hold the current filter length while switching from auto to manual filter mode (retains the auto filter setting).

commands.name: Offset
commands.description: Enter a dB offset for the active entry channel; the meter converts to and adds the equivalent value to the measurement. Signed value followed by EN. Out-of-range entries generate Error 51 (OS Error).

commands.name: Display Offset
commands.description: DSP - OFS: display the current offset value. Valid only when sent immediately after the OFFSET (OS) program code.

commands.name: Low Limit
commands.description: Set the low limit for the active entry channel limits-checking function. Signed value (sxxx.xxx) in dBm followed by EN.

commands.name: High Limit
commands.description: Set the high limit for the active entry channel limits-checking function. Signed value (sxxx.xxx) in dBm followed by EN.

commands.name: Enable Limits Checking
commands.description: Enable the limits-checking function using the current high and low limit values. PRESET disables limits checking.

commands.name: Disable Limits Checking
commands.description: Disable the limits-checking function.

commands.name: Log Units (dB/dBm)
commands.description: Select logarithmic display units: dBm for single-sensor/difference, dB for ratio/relative.

commands.name: Linear Units (Watts/%)
commands.description: Select linear display units: Watts for single-sensor/difference, percent for ratio/relative.

commands.name: Enter REL mode
commands.description: Enter relative measurement mode. The first reading taken becomes the reference; subsequent measurements are displayed relative to it.

commands.name: Exit REL mode
commands.description: Exit relative measurement mode.

commands.name: Reference Oscillator On
commands.description: Turn the internal 1.00 mW (0 dBm) 50 MHz power reference oscillator on.

commands.name: Reference Oscillator Off
commands.description: Turn the internal power reference oscillator off. PRESET default is off.

commands.name: Enter
commands.description: Terminate a numeric entry. Required after the value for any program code that takes numeric data (CL, KB, OS, RM, FM, LL, LH, ST, RC).

commands.name: Store
commands.description: Store the current instrument configuration into a register (1-19). Followed by register number and EN.

commands.name: Recall
commands.description: Recall an instrument configuration from a register (0-19). Register 0 holds the previous configuration (RECALL 0 recovers from an entry error and is executed at power-up). Followed by register number and EN.

commands.name: Preset
commands.description: Preset the Power Meter to its default state (auto range/filter both sensors, cal factor 100%, offset/limits 0, REL off, reference oscillator off, units Watts, free-run trigger). The Clear message (DCL/SDC) produces the same result.

commands.name: Display All
commands.description: Display all segments (front panel display test).

commands.name: Display Disable
commands.description: Disable (blank) the front panel display.

commands.name: Display Enable
commands.description: Enable (restore) the front panel display.

commands.name: Trigger Hold
commands.description: Trigger hold: stop taking measurements (hold the current measurement).

commands.name: Trigger Immediate
commands.description: Trigger immediate: take one measurement immediately (without waiting for the meter to settle) and output the result.

commands.name: Trigger with Delay
commands.description: Trigger with delay: take one settled measurement (wait for the meter to settle) and output the result.

commands.name: Trigger Free Run
commands.description: Free-run trigger: continuously take measurements. PRESET/power-up default trigger mode.

commands.name: GET response: Trigger Hold/Ignore
commands.description: Set the Group Execute Trigger (GET) bus command to be ignored.

commands.name: GET response: Trigger Immediate
commands.description: Set the meter to respond to the Group Execute Trigger (GET) bus command with Trigger Immediate (TR1).

commands.name: GET response: Trigger with Delay
commands.description: Set the meter to respond to the Group Execute Trigger (GET) bus command with Trigger with Delay (TR2). PRESET/turn-on default.

commands.name: Status Message
commands.description: Request the Status Message. After SM and when addressed to talk, the meter sends a string of 23 ASCII characters (followed by CR, LF, EOI) describing its current state. Updated only after a measurement.

commands.name: Clear Status Byte
commands.description: Clear the status byte (status bits remain set until the status byte is cleared).

commands.name: Service Request Mask
commands.description: Prefix for the Service Request Mask. Followed by an 8-bit byte (Data message) whose value is the sum of the weights of the status-byte condition bits (1-5) to be enabled for SRQ: Data Ready (1), Cal/Zero Complete (2), Entry Error (4), Measurement Error (8), Over/Under Limit (16).

commands.name: Service Request Mask Value
commands.description: Request the Service Request Mask Value. After RV and when addressed to talk, the meter returns 1 byte (EOI) giving the current mask value.

commands.name: Learn Mode #1
commands.description: Learn Mode #1: when addressed to talk after LP1, the meter outputs an ASCII string describing its current configuration (see Learn Mode #1 output format).

commands.name: Learn Mode #2
commands.description: Learn Mode #2: when addressed to talk after LP2, the meter outputs the 2 ASCII characters '@2' followed by 28 8-bit binary bytes (the last byte sent with EOI) describing its configuration. Requires a controller able to transfer binary data; the data can be returned to the meter to restore the configuration.

commands.name: Identification
commands.description: Identification query. When addressed to talk after ?ID, the meter returns 'HP438A,VERX.XX' (CR/LF, EOI), giving the model number and firmware version.
